title: The Duh Vinci Code

jokes.text: So, Fry, what do you do for a living? Me? Um... can I phone a friend?
jokes.setup: Host asks standard opening question on game show
jokes.punchline: Fry treats it like a game show question instead of chitchat
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Fry's confusion is genuine and fits his personality while subverting the format

jokes.text: I didn't come to play; I came to win. Now let's play.
jokes.setup: Fry declares he came to win, not play
jokes.punchline: Immediately says 'let's play' contradicting himself
jokes.explanation: Simple contradiction joke that fits Fry's character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: B: Nail! Final answer!
jokes.setup: Game show question presumably about hammers or tools
jokes.punchline: Fry confidently chooses 'Nail' as answer to wrong category
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry stupidity - confident wrong answer delivered with game show enthusiasm

jokes.text: Sweet dodo of Lesotho!
jokes.setup: Hermes needs an exclamation for Fry's wrong answer
jokes.punchline: Bizarre specific oath combining extinct bird and African country
jokes.explanation: Excellent Hermes-specific exclamation - absurd specificity that's perfectly in character

jokes.text: Don't you ever stop to think before you speak? I never stop to think about it.
jokes.setup: Hermes asks if Fry thinks before speaking
jokes.punchline: Fry admits he never thinks about thinking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry response - he doesn't even think about whether he thinks, showing depth of stupidity

jokes.text: His intelligence is just a little... differenty.
jokes.setup: Leela tries to diplomatically describe Fry's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Makes up word 'differenty' trying to be nice
jokes.explanation: Leela's attempt at politeness backfires with made-up word, showing her awkwardness with tact

jokes.text: (imitates Redd Foxx): You a big dummy!
jokes.setup: Discussion of Fry's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Bender does Sanford and Son reference
jokes.explanation: Simple pop culture reference without much added value - relies on recognition

jokes.text: You can barely remember your own name, Einstein. Einstein is a hard name to remember.
jokes.setup: Professor sarcastically calls Fry 'Einstein'
jokes.punchline: Fry takes it literally and agrees Einstein is hard to remember
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry misunderstanding - takes sarcastic 'Einstein' literally and finds reasonable justification

jokes.text: Don't have a shmaneurysm.
jokes.setup: Fry tells Professor not to have an aneurysm
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces it as 'shmaneurysm'
jokes.explanation: Typical Fry mispronunciation - adds 'shm' prefix unnecessarily, showing his verbal confusion

jokes.text: I'll be a monkey's uncle if I'm this monkey's nephew.
jokes.setup: Professor expresses disbelief about family relation
jokes.punchline: Twists common phrase to specifically reference their evolutionary relationship
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - common expression twisted to reference evolution and their actual relationship

jokes.text: Who will hug me if I achieve something?
jokes.setup: Fry realizes he has no family support
jokes.punchline: Pathetic question about who would celebrate his rare achievements
jokes.explanation: Touching character moment that's both funny and sad - reveals Fry's need for validation

jokes.text: What turned them to stone?
jokes.setup: Looking at marble busts of great thinkers
jokes.punchline: Fry thinks they were real people turned to stone
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry stupidity - doesn't understand that busts are sculptures, thinks they're petrified people

jokes.text: Uh-oh. Nibbler died in the wall.
jokes.setup: Strange discovery behind wall panel
jokes.punchline: Fry's first assumption is that pet died in wall
jokes.explanation: Fry jumps to morbid conclusion about mysterious discovery, showing his illogical thinking

jokes.text: Oh, I suppose if I have an Achilles' heel, it's because I bought it at that same auction.
jokes.setup: Professor mentions having an Achilles' heel (weakness)
jokes.punchline: Reveals he literally bought Achilles' actual heel at auction
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay taking idiom literally - Professor actually owns the physical heel, making the expression literal

jokes.text: Indeed so. Most indeededly.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg agrees with Professor's statement
jokes.punchline: Makes up word 'indeededly' to sound more intellectual
jokes.explanation: Zoidberg tries to sound smart by adding unnecessary suffix, typical of his pretentious tendencies

jokes.text: But possibly yes.
jokes.setup: Professor says 'No!' to something breaking
jokes.punchline: Immediately reconsiders with calm 'but possibly yes'
jokes.explanation: Professor's immediate reversal from dramatic 'No!' to casual acceptance shows his scattered nature

jokes.text: And now, at last, neither do I!
jokes.setup: Professor explains scholars didn't know what device was for
jokes.punchline: Proudly declares he also doesn't know
jokes.explanation: Professor's pride in his ignorance subverts expectation that he'd solve the mystery

jokes.text: Well, I may not have brain smarts, but at least I have street smarts.
jokes.setup: Fry acknowledges he lacks intelligence
jokes.punchline: Claims to have street smarts instead
jokes.explanation: Sets up for immediate contradiction - Fry's confidence before fall

jokes.text: I was in the hospital two weeks. No one visited me.
jokes.setup: Horn honk and thud sound effects after Fry claims street smarts
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was hit by car and abandoned
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to street smarts claim - he was hit by car AND nobody cared, doubling the failure

jokes.text: Not even a card.
jokes.setup: Fry already said no one visited him in hospital
jokes.punchline: Makes it worse by adding that no one sent a card either
jokes.explanation: Escalates the sadness of his situation - not only no visits but not even minimal effort of a card

jokes.text: Everybody at Kinko's was an idiot, so I just brought the original.
jokes.setup: Professor sent Bender to get copy of The Last Supper
jokes.punchline: Bender stole original painting instead of getting photocopy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender solution - theft disguised as problem-solving, completely in character and escalates absurdly

jokes.text: That's the great thing about that hand.
jokes.setup: Mysterious extra hand pointing knife at apostle
jokes.punchline: Fry finds the threatening gesture appealing
jokes.explanation: Fry's casual appreciation for violence is unexpectedly dark and fits his oblivious nature

jokes.text: My doctorate is in art history.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg displays unexpected knowledge about pentimento
jokes.punchline: Reveals he actually has relevant expertise
jokes.explanation: Excellent character revelation - subverts expectations about Zoidberg being completely incompetent

jokes.text: Wait a second! I'm not big-boned; I'm just fat.
jokes.setup: X-ray reveals what's under painting and Hermes sees himself
jokes.punchline: Hermes has honest revelation about his body
jokes.explanation: Hermes' moment of self-awareness about common euphemism for weight

jokes.text: My God! I'm looking!
jokes.setup: Professor says 'My God! Look!'
jokes.punchline: Leela matches his dramatic tone just to confirm she's looking
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation of dramatic exclamations - matches Professor's intensity for mundane confirmation

jokes.text: My God! This is the greatest mystery of all time.
jokes.setup: Series of dramatic 'My God!' exclamations
jokes.punchline: Fry caps it with grandiose assessment
jokes.explanation: Continues escalation pattern but Fry's hyperbole is predictable

jokes.text: Didn't we used to be a delivery company?
jokes.setup: Professor declares they must fly to Rome for investigation
jokes.punchline: Leela points out they've abandoned their actual job
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on how the show often abandons its premise for adventures - self-aware humor

jokes.text: PST! Leela, wanna join the mile-deep club?
jokes.setup: Underground in Roman catacombs
jokes.punchline: Bender proposes sex club based on depth instead of height
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender horniness adapted to location - mile-high club twisted for underground setting

jokes.text: Roman numerals?! I've got it! We're in Rome.
jokes.setup: Leela points out Roman numerals as clue
jokes.punchline: Fry deduces they're in Rome from Roman numerals
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - makes obvious connection and treats it as brilliant deduction

jokes.text: One... Okay, we're there.
jokes.setup: Professor counts paces according to ancient instructions
jokes.punchline: Fry declares journey complete after one step
jokes.explanation: Fry's impatience and lack of understanding of the process - wants instant results

jokes.text: I bet he's up in Robot Heaven right now. So he won't miss his eyes.
jokes.setup: Discovering Saint James was a robot
jokes.punchline: Bender justifies grave robbing by claiming victim is in heaven
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with twisted logic - Bender uses religious belief to justify theft, perfectly in character

jokes.text: My God! Robot Saint James is a zombie.
jokes.setup: Robot saint suddenly moves
jokes.punchline: Fry adds 'zombie' to already complex situation
jokes.explanation: Escalates already weird situation - robot + saint + zombie = comedy overload

jokes.text: Nay, I am not Saint James. Enough of your lies, Saint James.
jokes.setup: Robot denies being Saint James
jokes.punchline: Fry accuses him of lying about not being Saint James
jokes.explanation: Fry's stubborn refusal to accept correction - assumes robot is lying about his own identity

jokes.text: When I took repose in this coffin, I carefully tossed the real Saint James in yonder heap.
jokes.setup: Explaining how he ended up in Saint James's tomb
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions disposing of actual saint's body
jokes.explanation: Dark casual mention of body disposal - treats grave desecration as minor housekeeping

jokes.text: Ah, I told him I'd be back in five minutes. Ah, well, he'll make a nice heap.
jokes.setup: Animatronio mentions Pope John waiting
jokes.punchline: Casually accepts Pope died waiting, will join the body heap
jokes.explanation: Continues dark humor theme - casual attitude toward death and body disposal

jokes.text: (girlish squeal) Was he nice? How did his hair smell?
jokes.setup: Amy learns robot knew Da Vinci personally
jokes.punchline: Reacts like teenager meeting celebrity crush
jokes.explanation: Amy's shallow celebrity obsession applied to historical figure - perfectly fits her character

jokes.text: The what, now?
jokes.setup: Robot mentions shadow society
jokes.punchline: Fry's casual confusion about important plot point
jokes.explanation: Fry's characteristic response to complex information - casual dismissal of important details

jokes.text: Why does a robot need a codpiece?
jokes.setup: Interrogating the medieval robot
jokes.punchline: Leela asks practical question about robot anatomy
jokes.explanation: Smart observational humor - questions the logic of robot having medieval armor for genitals

jokes.text: Fie, thou fen-suckled bumbailey!
jokes.setup: Robot refuses to give information
jokes.punchline: Uses elaborate Shakespearean-style insult
jokes.explanation: Creative archaic insult that sounds authentic but is likely made up - great character voice

jokes.text: Trey... V... Trey... Trevi! It's the Trevi Fountain!
jokes.setup: Leela analyzes Neptune statue for clues
jokes.punchline: Makes logical leap to famous fountain
jokes.explanation: Decent puzzle-solving moment - shows Leela's intelligence but feels somewhat contrived

jokes.text: There can be no question! But Professor...? There can be no question!
jokes.setup: Professor declares certainty
jokes.punchline: Cuts off objection by repeating declaration
jokes.explanation: Professor's stubborn refusal to hear contradictions - typical of his character

jokes.text: What are you, senile? I'm not jumping in there. Hey, look... coins.
jokes.setup: Professor tells everyone to jump in fountain
jokes.punchline: Bender refuses then immediately enters for money
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender behavior - principled refusal immediately abandoned for profit

jokes.text: I got 48 cents.
jokes.setup: Underwater fight scene with guns and violence
jokes.punchline: Bender calmly reports small financial gain
jokes.explanation: Excellent understatement - treats dramatic action sequence as minor coin collection exercise

jokes.text: (gasps) There's one more nickel, and it's a big one!
jokes.setup: Bender already collected coins
jokes.punchline: Spots giant nickel worth pursuing despite danger
jokes.explanation: Continues Bender's money obsession - willing to risk life for larger coin

jokes.text: Note how the perspective lines draw the eye right to his dong.
jokes.setup: Professor admires Da Vinci's Vitruvian Man artwork
jokes.punchline: Academic analysis focuses on genitals
jokes.explanation: Combines high art appreciation with juvenile focus - Professor's academic tone makes it funnier

jokes.text: I mean... that flying machine's as aerodynamic as a sofa!
jokes.setup: Professor explaining why machines don't work
jokes.punchline: Specific comparison to furniture emphasizes poor design
jokes.explanation: Good specific comparison - sofa is unexpectedly accurate analogy for poor aerodynamics

jokes.text: In a way thou shalt ne'er discover!
jokes.setup: Professor says machine can't fly
jokes.punchline: Hidden robot dramatically contradicts him
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic entrance with information contradiction - sets up mystery

jokes.text: Porco metallico! Ne'er shall I reveal how these wondrous machines fit together!
jokes.setup: Robot refuses to give secrets
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals machines fit together while denying it
jokes.explanation: Classic 'saying too much' moment - robot reveals secret while trying to hide it

jokes.text: No wonder this contraption isn't aerodynamic! It's not an aircraft, it's a spacecraft!
jokes.setup: Machine reconfigures around Fry
jokes.punchline: Fry makes correct deduction for once
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of Fry intelligence - makes logical leap that others missed

jokes.text: I am Leonardo. guy.
jokes.setup: Dramatic reveal of Leonardo Da Vinci
jokes.punchline: Awkward casual delivery with unnecessary 'guy' addition
jokes.explanation: Anticlimactic introduction - undercuts dramatic moment with casual speech

jokes.text: That's what I was gonna say!
jokes.setup: Professor says 'My God!' at Leonardo reveal
jokes.punchline: Fry claims he would have said the same thing
jokes.explanation: Simple Fry moment - wants credit for obvious reaction

jokes.text: And might I say, you were great in Titanic. The Beach... eh.
jokes.setup: Fry expresses honor to meet Leonardo
jokes.punchline: Confuses Da Vinci with DiCaprio
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry confusion - mixes up historical figure with modern actor who played him

jokes.text: Heh. Looks like eating rocks wasn't as dumb as you said.
jokes.setup: Fry apparently ate rocks on alien planet
jokes.punchline: Claims vindication for seemingly stupid behavior
jokes.explanation: Fry finds justification for apparently stupid behavior - typical of his character

jokes.text: How's your football team? Learned.
jokes.setup: Fry asks about sports at university planet
jokes.punchline: Leonardo replies with academic assessment instead of wins/losses
jokes.explanation: Good culture clash - academic planet values learning over athletic performance

jokes.text: All this knowledge is giving me a raging 'brainer.'
jokes.setup: Professor excited by academic environment
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo applied to intellectual arousal
jokes.explanation: Obvious sexual pun - Professor's horniness for knowledge, but predictable

jokes.text: No. It would just go in one ear and our some other hole.
jokes.setup: Offered chance to attend math lecture
jokes.punchline: Corrupts 'in one ear, out the other' saying with crude anatomy
jokes.explanation: Fry mangles common expression with crude twist - fits his character but somewhat cheap

jokes.text: I have a terrible secret to confess, Mr. DiCaprio.
jokes.setup: Fry talking to Leonardo
jokes.punchline: Still thinks he's DiCaprio despite correction
jokes.explanation: Continues DiCaprio mistake despite correction - shows Fry doesn't listen

jokes.text: Duh! I'm Leonardo!
jokes.setup: Leonardo explains he's considered stupid on his planet
jokes.punchline: Uses 'duh' ironically while being the smartest human in history
jokes.explanation: Excellent irony - history's greatest genius using 'duh' because he's the dumb one on smart planet

jokes.text: Duh! I have to draw in pencil, 'cause I don't know how to use rendering software.
jokes.setup: Leonardo continues explaining his stupidity
jokes.punchline: Drawing skills are primitive compared to digital art
jokes.explanation: Brilliant perspective flip - Renaissance art techniques are primitive by future standards

jokes.text: Stupid Biff! Thinks he's so smart. He looks stronger than you, too.
jokes.setup: Leonardo points out another person
jokes.punchline: Adds insult about physical weakness to intellectual inadequacy
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation of insults - adds physical inadequacy to intellectual

jokes.text: I can see myself in your shiny button.
jokes.setup: Leonardo sharing personal story about being outcast
jokes.punchline: Fry distracted by reflective button
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - completely ignores emotional confession for shiny object

jokes.text: We had to answer every question in the form of an opera.
jokes.setup: Amy describes calculus lecture difficulty
jokes.punchline: Academic rigor includes artistic performance requirements
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of academic planet's excessive requirements - combines math and opera

jokes.text: I have a lot of homework for tomorrow, if I don't want to be... ♪ embarrassed. ♪
jokes.setup: Amy mentions homework
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates opera requirement by singing last word
jokes.explanation: Nice payoff to opera joke - shows Amy absorbed the planet's requirements

jokes.text: Ladies and gentlemen... Leonardo DiCaprio! I mean DaVinci!
jokes.setup: Announcer introducing Leonardo for show
jokes.punchline: Makes same DiCaprio mistake as Fry, then corrects
jokes.explanation: Continues DiCaprio running gag - even announcer makes same mistake

jokes.text: Nice hat! AUDIENCE: Ooh!
jokes.setup: Leonardo tries to show invention
jokes.punchline: Biff delivers weak insult that crowd finds devastating
jokes.explanation: Smart planet humor - mild insult is devastating because their standards for wit are higher

jokes.text: Wait... you told me it was an unstoppable ice cream machine.
jokes.setup: Leonardo reveals doomsday machine
jokes.punchline: Fry was told it made ice cream instead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Leonardo lied about machine's true purpose to get Fry's help

jokes.text: Ice cream is just a byproduct of the machine.
jokes.setup: Fry questions ice cream vs. doomsday machine
jokes.punchline: Leonardo admits death machine coincidentally makes ice cream
jokes.explanation: Dark humor - death machine happens to make ice cream as side effect

jokes.text: I hate these nerds! Just 'cause I'm stupider than them, they think they're smarter than me.
jokes.setup: Professor joins Leonardo's revenge plan
jokes.punchline: Illogical complaint about people correctly assessing intelligence
jokes.explanation: Excellent circular logic - Professor angry that smart people recognize they're smarter than him

jokes.text: I've got one single nail, and another nail to nail it in with
jokes.setup: Fry declares he'll stop the machine
jokes.punchline: Plans to use two nails, somehow nailing one with the other
jokes.explanation: Callback to opening game show - Fry's knowledge of nails becomes useful

jokes.text: Some doomsday machine! It barely killed anyone!
jokes.setup: Machine malfunctions and stops working
jokes.punchline: Bender disappointed by low death toll
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - disappointed by machine's failure to cause mass death

jokes.text: Poor Leonardo DaVinci. He sure was stupid.
jokes.setup: Leonardo killed by own machine
jokes.punchline: Professor calls history's greatest genius stupid
jokes.explanation: Ironic eulogy - Professor calls Leonardo stupid after his death by own invention

jokes.text: Your tiny, tiny intellect. Oops! There I go again, you dope. Uh, I mean, dummy.
jokes.setup: Professor tries to apologize to Fry
jokes.punchline: Keeps accidentally insulting him while apologizing
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor behavior - can't help insulting Fry even when trying to be nice

jokes.text: That's what my mom used to say. She was a wise woman. Also that I'm not much to look at.
jokes.setup: Fry says his mom called him good-hearted
jokes.punchline: Adds that she also said he was ugly
jokes.explanation: Good Fry moment - adds self-deprecating detail that undermines the compliment

jokes.text: I can't believe it! I'm gonna die! Next Thursday...
jokes.setup: Fry dramatically declares he's going to die
jokes.punchline: Adds specific day, making it sound like scheduled appointment
jokes.explanation: Anticlimactic ending - treats death like calendar appointment rather than tragedy

tmdb_overview: The Planet Express crew races to future Rome to unearth the shocking secret of Leonardo da Vinci.
